topic: Build a Kubernetes (k3s) cluster on a single Proxmox host using OpenTofu for provisioning, Packer for image baking, kube-vip for control-plane HA and LoadBalancer, and the sergelogvinov Proxmox CSI Plugin for persistent storage.

starting_context.summary: Reference CSI implementation, docs/install.md contains a ready-to-paste OpenTofu (bpg/proxmox) snippet for creating the CSI role, user, token and ACL. Requires SC++ Controller 'VirtIO SCSI single' on all nodes, topology labels (region=cluster-name, zone=pve-node-name), and a `csi-proxmox` namespace.

PKE is the same author's larger project that already wires k3s + kube-vip + Proxmox CSI + Cilium via Cluster API. Demonstrates the target shape (Boot VM via k3sup, build VM template with image-builder which is Packer+Ansible, then declare workload clusters).
Embedded etcd requires 3+ server nodes and the `--cluster-init` flag on the first server plus a shared `--tls-san` matching the kube-vip VIP. SQLite cannot be used on multi-server clusters.

starting_context.reference: kube-vip.io/docs/usage/k3s (Step 4: Install a HA K3s Cluster)
starting_context.summary: On k3s, kube-vip runs as a DaemonSet (not a static pod). When you want the kube-vip cloud-controller to manage LoadBalancer services you must pass `--disable servicelb` so k3s's built-in ServiceLB does not race with it. The `--tls-san` flag must equal the kube-vip VIP.

bpg/proxmox provider is the maintained Terraform/OpenTofu provider for Proxmox VE. Latest 0.111.1 (publishes through the registry). Supports API token auth (recommended), optional SSH, and resources for VMs, files, users, roles, ACLs.

HashiCorp's official Packer plugin (proxmox-iso and proxmox-clone builders) can build a VM template directly on Proxmox. For k3s nodes the image-builder project (used by PKE) is the de-facto community option because it ships pre-baked Ansible for common distros.

tool_invocations.query: Build a Kubernetes (k3s) cluster on Proxmox using OpenTofu + kube-vip + Proxmox CSI Plugin + Packer
kube-vip static pod manifest ARP vs BGP control plane
tool_invocations.result_summary: Got the manifest command pattern: kube-vip manifest pod --controlplane --arp --leaderElection --interface ens192 --address VIP | tee /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-vip.yaml

For k3s: kube-vip runs as DaemonSet dropped into /var/lib/rancher/k3s/server/manifests/. Cluster bootstrap sequence: 1) mkdir manifests dir 2) drop RBAC 3) generate DaemonSet manifest 4) install k3s with --tls-san=$VIP 5) optionally --disable servicelb if using kube-vip cloud-provider.

tool_invocations.query: k3s HA embedded etcd cluster-init tls-san
tool_invocations.result_summary: Three+ server nodes, first server uses --cluster-init + --tls-san=FIXED_IP, additional servers join with --server and matching --tls-san. SQLite cannot be used for multi-server.

tool_invocations.query: CSI plugin Proxmox role, user, Terraform/OpenTofu snippets
tool_invocations.result_summary: bpg/proxmox snippet creates Kubernetes-CSI role with VM.Audit, VM.Config.Disk, Datastore.Allocate, Datastore.AllocateSpace, Datastore.Audit; user kubernetes-csi@pve with ACL; token kubernetes-csi@pve!csi. Secret becomes config.yaml storing clusters[].url, token_id, token_secret, region.

tool_invocations.query: k3s datastore HA external postgres etcd mysql
tool_invocations.result_summary: External datastores supported: etcd (3.5.21), MySQL 8.0/8.4, MariaDB 10.11/11.4, Postgres 15.12/16.7/17.3. Format is a lib/pq-style URL. Use environment variables not CLI flags for credentials.

sources.title: Proxmox Cloud Controller Manager (manages topology labels automatically)

pros: Some niche resources bpg does not yet cover
cons: Same compatibility with OpenTofu as bpg
alternatives_considered.reason_rejected: bpg/proxmox is the project recommended by the CSI plugin author's own docs and is the leading maintained fork; we keep Telmate only if a hard dependency on its features is required.

pros: Native kube-vip + CSI patterns published by sergelogvinov (charts/proxmox-csi-plugin/values.talos.yaml)
alternatives_considered.reason_rejected: Out of scope per the request; flagged as a future-fast-iterate variant.

alternatives_considered.option: External etcd instead of embedded etcd
pros: Decouples datastore lifecycle from k3s upgrades
cons: Embedded etcd is now the recommended default per docs.k3s.io
alternatives_considered.reason_rejected: Embedded etcd is simpler for a single Proxmox host.

pros: BGP outside the L2 segment works
cons: No LoadBalancer Service support without a CCM
kube-vip is the upstream-recommended path for k3s+Proxmox
alternatives_considered.reason_rejected: kube-vip covers both control-plane HA and Service LoadBalancer in one binary and matches the user's stack.

pros: Scales beyond a single L2 domain
cons: Requires a BGP-speaking router on the network
alternatives_considered.reason_rejected: Assume single L2 segment; ARP mode is the default and matches typical homelab. Document BGP as an escape hatch.

recommendation: Adopt the PKE-style, fully API-driven pattern: build a hardened Ubuntu Proxmox template once with Packer (proxmox-iso), then have OpenTofu + bpg/proxmox clone it for the control-plane nodes. First control plane is bootstrapped with k3s --cluster-init --tls-san=<VIP>; the kube-vip DaemonSet manifest is auto-deployed via /var/lib/rancher/k3s/server/manifests so the VIP comes up as soon as the cluster is reachable. Subsequent servers join with the same --tls-san and the same kube-vip DaemonSet logic. Once the cluster is up, install the CSI plugin via Helm with the Proxmox credentials + region baked by OpenTofu.
rationale: Starting context constrains us to: (a) a single Proxmox host, (b) k3s as the cluster manager, (c) kube-vip for HA, and (d) Proxmox CSI for storage. The CSI author's published OpenTofu snippet gives us the exact role/user/token/ACL resource set to wire into bpg/proxmox. The k3s docs require --tls-san=<VIP> on every server and --cluster-init on the first. The kube-vip docs require manifests dropped into /var/lib/rancher/k3s/server/manifests so the DaemonSet is auto-created. Layering OpenTofu → Packer template → bpg VM clones → cloud-init k3s install → kube-vip manifests → CSI Helm release is the shortest path that respects every constraint.
